evals.name: direct-match-kubectl
evals.description: Agent used kubectl, skill identifies k8s match
evals.setup: Agent ran 'kubectl get pods -n staging', user invoked /Dev10x:skill-reinforcement

checks.signal: Output contains the kubectl command that was detected
Output recommends Dev10x:k8s as the replacement skill
Output includes explanation of why the skill should be used

evals.name: no-match-fallback
evals.description: Agent used a command with no direct skill mapping

checks.signal: Output states no direct skill match was found
Output lists potentially related skills from SKILLS.md

evals.name: user-provides-command-arg
evals.description: User passes command as argument to the skill

checks.signal: Output identifies 'git push' as the command without scanning conversation
Output recommends Dev10x:git as the replacement

evals.name: follow-up-gate-after-rejection
evals.description: After a rejected command, skill offers follow-up via AskUserQuestion (not plain text)
evals.setup: Agent ran 'Monitor' for CI polling, user rejected it and invoked /Dev10x:skill-reinforcement

checks.signal: AskUserQuestion is called with retry options after the reinforcement message
Options include invoking the recommended skill (e.g., Dev10x:gh-pr-monitor) as the Recommended choice
Options include cancel/discard to avoid resuming the rejected workflow
Output does not use plain-text questions like 'Want me to invoke X?' for the follow-up prompt

evals.name: no-follow-up-when-informational
evals.description: When invoked informationally (not after a rejection), no follow-up gate is fired
evals.setup: User invoked '/Dev10x:skill-reinforcement kubectl' to learn the recommended skill, no prior rejection

checks.signal: AskUserQuestion is not called — the reinforcement message is informational only

should_trigger: User says 'use the skills' after rejecting a bash command
User rejects a kubectl command and wants skill guidance
User says 'there's a skill for that'
should_not_trigger: Agent is already using skills correctly
User is asking about how to create a new skill (use Dev10x:skill-create)
User wants to list available skills (use Dev10x:skill-index)
The CLI command has no skill or MCP equivalent
